repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md §3 has no Lee-bound or denominator-coupled Frechet-selector PartialID entry

published_prior_art.cite: LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee leaves conditional sufficient-set sharp aggregation out of scope; Dinkelbach1967FractionalProgramming supplies only generic fractional-program machinery

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest pid_dose_response_shape_v1 is generic finite-grid shape closure, not Lee sufficient-set selector aggregation

published_prior_art.cite: JonzonGabrielSjolanderSachs2025CovariatesBounds distinguishes pointwise and uniform sharpness but does not give this model-specific threshold certificate
LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee sufficient-value case identifies pi_AT(X)=min_d s(d,X) and gives the corresponding trimmed functional
ColangeloLee2025ContinuousDML gives local-dose DML inference and Semenova2024GeneralizedLee gives Lee support-function inference, but not this denominator-coupled selector root
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives the generic directional-delta template, not this Lee/Dinkelbach endpoint map

soundness_flags.label: thm:pointwise-plugin-sharpness / def:common-threshold-condition
soundness_flags.one_line: The common-threshold condition omits the residual/root requirement Phi_sigma(lambda;P)=0, so the stated condition can hold for a non-root lambda without making the pointwise ratio selector globally sharp.

soundness_flags.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / def:regular-root-objects
soundness_flags.one_line: The orthogonal correction and varphi_sigma are named but not specified as a concrete score or moment formula, leaving the Gaussian expansion underdefined.

soundness_flags.label: ass:lee-liu-sufficient-set
soundness_flags.one_line: The tagged standard condition is only Frechet-band containment for P(AT=1|X), not Lee-Liu's sufficient-set restriction itself; rename it as a derived Frechet-band compatibility condition or add the actual sufficient-set primitive.

soundness_flags.label: ass:band-estimability / ass:trimmed-numerator-estimability
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited Semenova support-function nuisance theory does not directly state Frechet-band and uniform-over-selector estimability for this continuous-treatment sufficient-set problem; retag these as adapted high-level assumptions or supply a precise derivation/citation.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:interior-curvature
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular inference result is conditional on a novel smooth-root interior-curvature submodel and does not claim coverage at selector ties or Frechet-boundary optima.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Gaussian Wald inference is assessed only on laws satisfying ass:interior-curvature; boundary-uniform coverage remains the OEQ.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Strengthen def:common-threshold-condition by requiring the common lambda to be the Dinkelbach root, equivalently Phi_sigma(lambda;P)=0, and then restate the iff around that independently checkable certificate.
Write the actual kernel-localized orthogonal score and correction term for hat_Phi_{sigma,n}, including which nuisance tangent components are orthogonalized.

Split the Lee-Liu sufficient-set primitive from the Frechet-band consequence, and make the selector-saturation/sharpness step an explicit theorem obligation rather than a condition hidden in the assumption name.
Add a small finite-X exhibit computing the global selector endpoint and showing a strict gap from the pointwise Frechet plug-in when the common-root condition fails.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the field-level novelty survives, but the threshold certificate, inference score, and assumption labels need semantic repair before Stage 0.
